reason: The gate hit a stdout buffer overflow on attempt 1, then failed with truncated output on attempts 2 and 3. The first attempt revealed two real issues I fixed: (1) format — collapsed the run_turn call to a single line since it fit under 100 chars, (2) lint F841 — dropped the unused `sprite =` assignment from `resume_session` call. Those were committed in the second commit. The remaining gate failures on attempt 1 were pre-existing infrastructure issues unrelated to my changes: `security-deps` (CVE-2026-3219 in pip itself, not my code) and `test` (DB connection refused — no Postgres running in the check environment). Attempts 2 and 3 produced only truncated output with no gate details, suggesting the harness itself was hitting buffer limits and failing before any gate results could be reported.
summary_of_work:
Made all required changes on branch `claude/drop-sprite-arg-from-run-turn`:

Commit 1 (`df16426`): In `session_service/turn.py` — removed `sprite: Sprite` param, dropped `from sprites import Sprite` import, replaced 4-line docstring with one-liner, updated the single call site in `views/sessions.py` to drop the `sprite` positional arg.

Commit 2 (`f302f32`): Fixed the two gate failures revealed by attempt 1 — dropped unused `sprite =` assignment from `resume_session(...)` call (lint F841), and collapsed the run_turn call to a single line matching ruff format output (94 chars, under 100 limit).

All three acceptance criteria that can be verified statically pass: `run_turn` no longer accepts `sprite`; the `from sprites import Sprite` import is gone; the docstring no longer mentions "drop in a follow-up" or `sprite`.
